content.Text: [Mon 2026-04-20 08:37 PDT] Create an "Incident Post-Mortem Generator" dashboard page for this AgentLens observability tool. This is a standalone HTML page that auto-generates structured post-mortem reports from incidents. ## What to build Create `dashboard/postmortem.html` - an interactive incident post-mortem generator with: 1. **Incident Input Panel** - Form to define an incident: - Incident title, severity (P0-P4), affected agents (multi-select), start/end time - Impact description textarea - "Auto-Detect" button that simulates scanning telemetry data 2. **Timeline Reconstruction** - Auto-generated incident timeline: - Chronological event list with timestamps and severity markers - Canvas-rendered timeline visualization bar with color-coded phases (detection → triage → mitigation → resolution) - Click events to expand details 3. **Automated Root Cause Analysis** - AI-style analysis engine: - Analyzes the incident pattern and suggests top 3 probable root causes with confidence scores - Shows contributing factors as a fishbone/Ishikawa diagram (simplified Canvas rendering) - "Deep Dive" button for each cause that expands analysis 4. **Impact Assessment Dashboard**: - Affected agents/sessions count - Error rate spike percentage - Estimated downtime - User impact score (1-100) - Cost impact estimation - Canvas-rendered impact radar chart (5 dimensions) 5. **Action Items Generator** - Proactive recommendations: - Auto-generates 5-8 categorized action items (immediate, short-term, long-term) - Each has priority (P0-P3), owner assignment dropdown, due date - Status tracking (open/in-progress/done) - "Generate Prevention Plan" button that creates a follow-up prevention strategy 6. **Report Export**: - Full post-mortem as Markdown - JSON structured export - Print-friendly view - One-click copy to clipboard 7. **Post-Mortem Templates**: - 4 presets: "Service Outage", "Performance Degradation", "Data Pipeline Failure", "Security Incident" - Each preset pre-fills realistic demo data 8. **Post-Mortem History** - localStorage persistence: - Save/load past post-mortems - Compare two post-mortems side by side - Trend analysis: "Are incidents getting less severe over time?" ## Design Requirements - Match the existing dashboard style: use `styles.css` from the same directory - Dark theme by default with theme toggle support (reuse existing toggleTheme pattern) - Include the same header navigation bar as other pages (look at index.html for the nav pattern, add a "📝 Post-Mortem" link) - Fully self-contained HTML with inline JS (no external dependencies except styles.css) - Use Canvas API for visualizations (timeline bar, fishbone diagram, impact radar) - Responsive layout ## Navigation Update Add a nav link for post-mortem in index.html header. Add it after the "📋 Runbooks" link: ```html 📝 Post-Mortem ``` Add this same link in ALL other dashboard HTML files in their header nav sections too (alerts.html, command-center.html, correlator.html, cost-anomaly.html, costs.html, dependencies.html, diff.html, errors.html, profiler.html, replay.html, runbooks.html, scorecards.html, sla.html, timeline.html, waterfall.html). ## Build verification After writing all files, verify with: `python -m py_compile agentlens/sdk.py` (just to make sure existing Python files still parse). Also open postmortem.html and check it's valid HTML structure. ## Git Commit all changes with message: "feat(dashboard): add Incident Post-Mortem Generator - auto-generates structured post-mortem reports with timeline reconstruction, root cause analysis, impact assessment, action items, 4 presets, Canvas visualizations, export to Markdown/JSON" Then push: `git push origin HEAD:main`
